title: Unlocking the Sun's Secrets: AI Analyzes 100 Years of Solar Activity Sketches
description: India News: Discover how artificial intelligence is transforming century-old solar observations into valuable scientific data, shedding light on long-term solar activity and space weather predictions.
content: BENGALURU: Artificial Intelligence has helped scientists unlock a century of the Sun’s history by analysing hand-drawn observations from the Kodaikanal Solar Observatory, turning fragile paper records into a valuable digital archive that could improv... [3153 chars]

title: The Dow hits a record as most of Wall Street rises, but slumping AI stocks keep indexes mixed
description: NEW YORK — Most US stocks rose on Thursday, and the Dow Jones Industrial Average rallied to another record, but more drops for computer chip companies and other winners of the artificial-intelligence boom kept indexes mixed.
content:

description: Microsoft Corp. is setting up a new organization with 6,000 employees to help businesses with the technical and strategic work of deploying artificial intelligence.

title: Can caged frontier AI models outrun open ones?
description: For most of the past two years, Washington’s approach to artificial intelligence has been simple: let the labs run. That changed on June 12, when the Commerce Department classified Anthropic’s newest models as dual-use cyber tools
content:
Anthropic made its most advanced AI model, Fable, generally available earlier in June, sparking a cautionary tale for American AI companies.
Three days after launch, the company suspended access for its paid users over concerns that hackers had circu... [5330 chars]

title: AI race weakens climate pledges at Google, Amazon
description: Google and Amazon this week reported sharp increases in greenhouse gas emissions, driven by the frantic construction of artificial intelligence infrastructure that is pushing the tech giants further from their carbon neutrality pledges. Amazon's emissions, published on Wednesday, have risen 58 percent over the same period, and more than 16 percent last year, despite a pledge to reach carbon neutrality by 2040.

title: Is AI moving too fast to regulate? UN report flags global safety and rising risks
description: United Nations scientific panel’s first global report warns that artificial intelligence is evolving faster than governments can regulate or keep up with. While AI is driving breakthroughs in healthcare, education and research, experts also raise concerns over cyber threats, disinformation and weak global oversight. Research Professor Aniruddha Desai from La Trobe University says users must understand what AI tools can and cannot do.
